summary: Implementation is clean, well-structured, and follows established project patterns. Separation of utilities from the React component enables testability without a DOM. No critical issues. Three minor/major findings below.

findings.title: Unsafe type cast for `f.type` from Prisma — use FieldType union instead of raw `as` cast
findings.description: The field type coming from Prisma is stored as a plain string in the DB (Prisma enum maps to string at runtime). The code casts it with `f.type as 'TEXT' | 'EMAIL' | ...`. If a legacy/corrupt row has an unexpected value, the cast silently succeeds and the data reaches the editor in a broken state. The correct approach is to validate the value against the FieldType union and throw / skip bad rows, or re-use the Zod enum already defined in the API layer.
findings.recommendation: Import `FieldType` and validate: `const FIELD_TYPES = new Set(['TEXT','EMAIL','URL','NUMBER','TEXTAREA','FILE','CHECKBOX']); if (!FIELD_TYPES.has(f.type)) throw new Error(...)`. Alternatively, since Prisma 7 can be configured to use native enums, verify schema alignment. At minimum, wrap in a utility function rather than an inline `as` cast spread across multiple lines.

findings.title: Save errors are thrown but never surfaced to the user in the New template flow
findings.description: In `NewTemplatePage`, when `fetch` returns a non-ok response the handler throws `new Error(...)`. The `TemplateEditor.handleSave` wraps `onSave` in a try/finally, so the error is swallowed after `setSaving(false)` — no error message is shown to the user. The Edit flow (EditTemplatePage) correctly sets `saveError` state and renders it before calling `throw`. The New flow lacks this.
findings.recommendation: Add `const [saveError, setSaveError] = useState<string | null>(null)` to `NewTemplatePage` and render it above `<TemplateEditor>`, matching the pattern in `EditTemplatePage`. Alternatively, propagate error display into `TemplateEditor` itself via a new `saveError` prop.

findings.title: Duplicate import statements for the same module
findings.description: `SavePayload` and `FieldType` are both imported from `@/components/dashboard/TemplateEditorUtils` but in two separate `import type` lines. This is a trivial style issue but clutters the import block.
findings.recommendation: Merge into one: `import type { SavePayload, FieldType } from '@/components/dashboard/TemplateEditorUtils';`

findings.title: Validation runs on every render; `validationError` state is redundant with derived `validError`
findings.description: `validateTemplate(name, steps)` is called unconditionally on every render to produce `validError`, which is then used to disable the Save button. There is also `validationError` state that is only populated after a user clicks Save. These two paths are parallel: `validError !== null` already captures whether saving should be blocked. The `validationError` state is only used to display the error message — but since `validError` contains the same string, both could be unified.
findings.recommendation: Remove the `validationError` state. Show the error message using `validError` directly — but only after the first save attempt (gate it with a `hasAttemptedSave` boolean state). This simplifies state management without changing UX.

findings.title: Type re-declaration in server page duplicates TemplateEditorProps shape
findings.description: The `initialData` shape is declared inline inside the server page (lines 36–57) as a local mapping, while an identical type already exists as `TemplateEditorProps['initialData']` inside `TemplateEditor.tsx`. Changes to the type must be updated in two places.
findings.recommendation: Export `TemplateEditorInitialData` from `TemplateEditorUtils.ts` or from `TemplateEditor.tsx`, then import and use it in the page. This ensures the server-component mapping and the client component stay in sync.

positive_observations: Clean separation of pure logic into TemplateEditorUtils.ts — enables unit testing without a DOM and avoids mixing state and computation.
NEXTAUTH_URL correctly passed server-side as prop to client component — matches spec requirement and avoids Railway reverse-proxy issues.
Clipboard fallback with window.alert is correctly guarded in try/catch.
isProtected field delete button is hidden (not just disabled) exactly per spec.
force-dynamic correctly applied to all three server pages.
Session guard pattern matches the existing dashboard pattern exactly.
verdict: APPROVE WITH FIXES — CR-2 (save error not surfaced in New page) and CR-1 (type cast) should be fixed before deploy. CR-3, CR-4, CR-5 are optional but recommended.
